title: Wall Street’s Hunt for Cheaper Stocks Goes Global
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: Wall Street is increasingly looking abroad for cheaper stocks due to high valuations and a weak dollar. This trend reflects a shift in investment strategies as
excerpt: Wall Street is increasingly looking abroad for cheaper stocks due to high valuations and a weak dollar. This trend reflects a shift in investment strategies as firms seek better opportunities outside the U.S. market.

content.introduction: Wall Street is increasingly looking abroad for cheaper stocks due to high valuations and a weak dollar. This trend reflects a shift in investment strategies as firms seek better opportunities outside the U.S. market.
body: The Wall Street Journal Shop. Hunt for Cheaper Stocks Goes Global | The Wall Street Journal -- Tue., February 10, 2026. Adding product to your cart. Full newsprint issue of the Eastern Edition of The Wall Street Journal as published on Tuesday, February 10, 2026. **Top Story:** Hunt for Cheaper Stocks Goes Global - High valuations, weak dollar boost bets that U.S. market lead will contract. * In Subzero Cold, Green Berets Train for Arctic War. * Trump to Upend Emission Rules, Repealing Key Climate Finding. * Chinese Factory in U.S. Clobbers Rivals. * Job Seekers Are Paying Recruiters For Help. * Inside OpenAI Leaders’ Decision to Take Down A Beloved AI Model. * A-Hed: Couples Brace for the Apocalypse: A Super Bowl on Valentine’s Day. Use left/right arrows to navigate the slideshow or swipe left/right if using a mobile device. * Choosing a selection results in a full page refresh. * Press the space key then arrow keys to make a selection.
Last spring, it was “Sell America.” Now Wall Street's hot trade is buy everywhere else. Wall Street's Hunt for Cheaper Stocks Goes Global.
